redcap:Reports.title: ECMELLA und Impella bridge to LVAD

ItemGroupDef.Name:
Pre-ECLS Arterial Blood Gas
1. Drawn prior to the ECLS Start Time
2. Drawn no more than 6 hours before the ECLS Start Time
3. If multiple arterial blood gases exist in this time period, choose the pre-ECLS arterial blood gas closest to AND before the ECLS Start Time
4. If the patient is on cardiopulmonary bypass immediately preceding ECLS please use a blood gas prior to cardiopulmonary bypass

Pre-ECLS Ventilator Settings
If multiple ventilator settings exist, report the last reported ventilator settings before the ECLS Start Time. If the patient was receiving hand delivered bag valve ventilation immediately prior to going on ECMO, please use the ventilator settings just prior to hand delivered bag valve ventilation unless the patient was hand delivered bag valve ventilation for the entire time prior to the ECLS Start Time.

Labor
1. Drawn prior to the ECLS Start Time
2. Drawn no more than 6 hours before the ECLS Start Time
3. If multiple values exist in this time period, choose the pre-ECLS labor closest to AND before the ECLS Start Time
4. If the patient is on cardiopulmonary bypass immediately preceding ECLS please use labor prior to cardiopulmonary bypass

Place where ECLS received

German Heart Center: Surgical ICU or Operating Room

Different hospital: best fitting type

Pre-Impella Arterial Blood Gas
1. Drawn prior to the Impella Start Time
2. Drawn no more than 6 hours before the Impella Start Time
3. If multiple arterial blood gases exist in this time period, choose the pre arterial blood gas closest to AND before the Impella Start Time
4. If the patient is on cardiopulmonary bypass immediately preceding Impella please use a blood gas prior to cardiopulmonary bypass

Pre-Impella Ventilator Settings
If multiple ventilator settings exist, report the last reported ventilator settings before the Impella Start Time.

Labor
1. Drawn prior to the Impella Start Time
3. If multiple values exist in this time period, choose the labor closest to AND before the Impella Start Time
4. If the patient is on cardiopulmonary bypass immediately preceding Impella please use labor prior to cardiopulmonary bypass

ECLS and Impella support in the last 24 hours ->

ItemDef.Name: limb_perf_necessary
ItemDef.redcap:FieldNote: arterial cannula in femoral vessel

Question.TranslatedText: Limb Perfusion present at ECLS Start

Mechanical replacement: Blood pump replaced for failure or suspected failure of normal mechanical operation Thrombosis / coagulopathy: Device exchange primarily indicated by clot burden or coagulation derangement Hemolysis: Device exchange primarily indicated by center-specific markers of hemolysis (for example, plasma free hemoglobin, lactate dehydrogenase, haptoglobin or bilirubin) Equipment longevity / center protocol: Device exchange indicated by center-specific protocol regarding longevity of use without evidence of other derangement. May be due to transition to or from a transport ECLS circuit. Entire circuit replaced due to indicated component(s) change: Device was exchanged as part of whole circuit exchange primarily for an indication specific to a circuit component other than the (membrane lung or blood pump) Entire circuit replaced following temporary transition to bypass: Device exchanged during whole circuit exchange following temporary transition of patient mechanical support o cardiopulmonary bypass within a continuous ECLS run
Question.redcap:FormattedTranslatedText:
Mechanical replacement: Blood pump replaced for failure or suspected failure of normal mechanical operation
Thrombosis / coagulopathy: Device exchange primarily indicated by clot burden or coagulation derangement
Hemolysis: Device exchange primarily indicated by center-specific markers of hemolysis (for example, plasma free hemoglobin, lactate dehydrogenase, haptoglobin or bilirubin)
Equipment longevity / center protocol: Device exchange indicated by center-specific protocol regarding longevity of use without evidence of other derangement. May be due to transition to or from a transport ECLS circuit.
Entire circuit replaced due to indicated component(s) change: Device was exchanged as part of whole circuit exchange primarily for an indication specific to a circuit component other than the (membrane lung or blood pump)
Entire circuit replaced following temporary transition to bypass: Device exchanged during whole circuit exchange following temporary transition of patient mechanical support o cardiopulmonary bypass within a continuous ECLS run

Question.TranslatedText: Structural integrity: Membrane lung exchanged for suspected impaired structural integrity Decreased efficiency of gas exchange: Membrane lung exchanged for the primary reason of compromised oxygenation and/or ventilation Acute obstruction to blood flow: Membrane lung exchanged in response to a sudden loss or significant decrease in blood flow or acute spike in transmembrane pressure within the circuit Increasing resistance to blood flow: Membrane lung exchanged in response to increasing trans-membrane pressures Thrombosis / coagulopathy: Device exchange primarily indicated by clot burden or coagulation derangement. Hemolysis: Device exchange primarily indicated by center-specific markers of hemolysis (for example, plasma free hemoglobin, lactate dehydrogenase, haptoglobin or bilirubin) Equipment longevity / center protocol: Device exchange indicated by center-specific protocol regarding longevity of use without evidence of other derangement. May be due to transition to or from a transport ECLS circuit. Entire circuit replaced due to indicated component(s) change: Device was exchanged as part of whole circuit exchange primarily for an indication specific to a circuit component other than the (membrane lung or blood pump) Entire circuit replaced following temporary transition to bypass: Device exchanged during whole circuit exchange following temporary transition of patient mechanical support o cardiopulmonary bypass within a continuous ECLS run
Question.redcap:FormattedTranslatedText:
Structural integrity: Membrane lung exchanged for suspected impaired structural integrity
Decreased efficiency of gas exchange: Membrane lung exchanged for the primary reason of compromised oxygenation and/or ventilation
Acute obstruction to blood flow: Membrane lung exchanged in response to a sudden loss or significant decrease in blood flow or acute spike in transmembrane pressure within the circuit
Increasing resistance to blood flow: Membrane lung exchanged in response to increasing trans-membrane pressures
Thrombosis / coagulopathy: Device exchange primarily indicated by clot burden or coagulation derangement.
Hemolysis: Device exchange primarily indicated by center-specific markers of hemolysis (for example, plasma free hemoglobin, lactate dehydrogenase, haptoglobin or bilirubin)
Equipment longevity / center protocol: Device exchange indicated by center-specific protocol regarding longevity of use without evidence of other derangement. May be due to transition to or from a transport ECLS circuit.
Entire circuit replaced due to indicated component(s) change: Device was exchanged as part of whole circuit exchange primarily for an indication specific to a circuit component other than the (membrane lung or blood pump)
Entire circuit replaced following temporary transition to bypass: Device exchanged during whole circuit exchange following temporary transition of patient mechanical support o cardiopulmonary bypass within a continuous ECLS run

ItemDef.redcap:SectionHeader:
Pre-Impella Arterial Blood Gas
1. Drawn prior to the Impella Start Time

4. If the patient is on cardiopulmonary bypass immediately preceding Impella please use a blood gas prior to cardiopulmonary bypass

Question.redcap:FormattedTranslatedText:
Pump Upgrade: Blood pump replaced for more efficient blood pump (Change Impella CP to Impella 5.5)
Mechanical replacement: Blood pump replaced for failure or suspected failure of normal mechanical operation
Thrombosis / coagulopathy: Device exchange primarily indicated by clot burden or coagulation derangement
Hemolysis: Device exchange primarily indicated by center-specific markers of hemolysis (for example, plasma free hemoglobin, lactate dehydrogenase, haptoglobin or bilirubin)
Equipment longevity / center protocol: Device exchange indicated by center-specific protocol regarding longevity of use without evidence of other derangement. 
Entire circuit replaced following temporary transition to bypass: Device exchanged during whole circuit exchange following temporary transition of patient mechanical support to cardiopulmonary bypass

ItemDef.redcap:FieldNote: peripheral cannulation site bleeding requiring >3 Units PRBC transfusion/day or surgical intervention (includes intravascular hemostatic agent deployment)

ItemDef.Name: media_cannulation_bleeding
ItemDef.redcap:FieldNote: mediastinal cannulation site bleeding requiring >3 Units PRBC transfusion/day or surgical intervention

ItemDef.Name: surgical_bleeding
ItemDef.redcap:FieldNote: requriring >3 Units PRBC/day or surgical intervention

ItemDef.Name: hemorrhage_o
ItemDef.redcap:FieldNote: requriring >3 Units PRBC/day or intervention

ItemDef.Name: cns_diffuse_ischemia
ItemDef.redcap:FieldNote: CT or MR demonstrating diffuse ischemic changes

ItemDef.Name: cns_local_ischemia
ItemDef.redcap:FieldNote: CT or MR demonstrating localized ischemic change

ItemDef.Name: cns_hemorrhage
ItemDef.redcap:FieldNote: may be intraparenchymal, subdural or subarachnoid

ItemDef.Name: neurosurgical_intervention
ItemDef.redcap:FieldNote: neurosurgical procedure performed during ECLS run (intracranial pressure monitor, external ventricular drain, craniotomy)

ItemDef.Name: cardio_complications
ItemDef.redcap:FieldNote: CPR, Tamponade, Cardiac Arrhythmia

ItemDef.Name: cpr
ItemDef.redcap:FieldNote: chest compressions and cardiopulmonary resuscitation required during tMCS run

ItemDef.Name: tamponade_blood
ItemDef.redcap:FieldNote: tamponade during tMCS run requiring pericardial drain or mediastinal washout

ItemDef.Name: tamponade_not
ItemDef.redcap:FieldNote: tamponade during ECLS run requiring pericardial drain or mediastinal washout

ItemDef.Name: cardiac_arrhythmia
ItemDef.redcap:FieldNote: requiring antiarrythmic medication infusion, overdrive pacing, cardioversion or defibrillation

ItemDef.Name: pulmonary_complications
ItemDef.redcap:FieldNote: Pneumothorax, Pulmonary Hemorrhage

ItemDef.Name: hemothorax
ItemDef.redcap:FieldNote: requriring >3 Units PRBC/day

ItemDef.Name: pneumothorax
ItemDef.redcap:FieldNote: requiring insertion of chest drain

ItemDef.Name: pulmonary_hemorrhage
ItemDef.redcap:FieldNote: requiring >3 Units PRBC/day

ItemDef.Name: limb_complications
ItemDef.redcap:FieldNote: Fasciotomy, Limb amputation, Limb ischemia requiring limb reperfusion cannula, Limb compartment syndrome

ItemDef.Name: fasciotomy
ItemDef.redcap:FieldNote: fasciotomy performed secondary to compartment syndrome from ECLS cannulation ( fasciotomy performed during ECLS hospitalization)

ItemDef.Name: amputation
ItemDef.redcap:FieldNote: limb amputation secondary to complications from ECLS run (amputation performed during ECLS hospitalization)

ItemDef.Name: limb_cannula
ItemDef.redcap:FieldNote: post peripheral cannulation, requiring addition of limb reperfusion cannula >= 6 hours post cannulation
